<task id="_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow" internal="true">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER skip a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution without fail or excuse</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content, discuss with the user the section completed, and NEVER proceed until the users indicates
      to proceed (unless YOLO mode has been activated)</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step in Order">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs and the workflow.xml runner</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>invoke-protocol name="protocol_name" xml tag → Execute reusable protocol from protocols section</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle template-output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask> [a] Advanced Elicitation, [c] Continue, [p] Party-Mode, [y] YOLO the rest of this document only. WAIT for response. <if
              response="a">
              <action>Start the advanced elicitation workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/advanced-elicitation/workflow.xml</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="c">
              <action>Continue to next step</action>
            </if>
            <if response="p">
              <action>Start the party-mode workflow {project-root}/_bmad/core/workflows/party-mode/workflow.md</action>
            </if>
            <if
              response="y">
              <action>Enter #yolo mode for the rest of the workflow</action>
            </if>
          </ask>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction and confirmation of EVERY step at EVERY template output - NO EXCEPTIONS except yolo MODE</mode>
    <mode name="yolo">Skip all confirmations and elicitation, minimize prompts and try to produce all of the workflow automatically by
      simulating the remaining discussions with an simulated expert user</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (ALWAYS wait for response before continuing)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
      <tag>invoke-protocol - Execute a reusable protocol (e.g., discover_inputs)</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <protocols desc="Reusable workflow protocols that can be invoked via invoke-protocol tag">
    <protocol name="discover_inputs" desc="Smart file discovery and loading based on input_file_patterns">
      <objective>Intelligently load project files (whole or sharded) based on workflow's input_file_patterns configuration</objective>

      <critical>Only execute if workflow.yaml contains input_file_patterns section</critical>

      <flow>
        <step n="1" title="Parse Input File Patterns">
          <action>Read input_file_patterns from loaded workflow.yaml</action>
          <action>For each pattern group (prd, architecture, epics, etc.), note the load_strategy if present</action>
        </step>

        <step n="2" title="Load Files Using Smart Strategies">
          <iterate>For each pattern in input_file_patterns:</iterate>

          <substep n="2a" title="Try Sharded Documents First">
            <check if="sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Determine load_strategy from pattern config (defaults to FULL_LOAD if not specified)</action>

              <strategy name="FULL_LOAD">
                <desc>Load ALL files in sharded directory - used for PRD, Architecture, UX, brownfield docs</desc>
                <action>Use glob pattern to find ALL .md files (e.g., "{output_folder}/*architecture*/*.md")</action>
                <action>Load EVERY matching file completely</action>
                <action>Concatenate content in logical order (index.md first if exists, then alphabetical)</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="SELECTIVE_LOAD">
                <desc>Load specific shard using template variable - example: used for epics with {{epic_num}}</desc>
                <action>Check for template variables in sharded_single pattern (e.g., {{epic_num}})</action>
                <action>If variable undefined, ask user for value OR infer from context</action>
                <action>Resolve template to specific file path</action>
                <action>Load that specific file</action>
                <action>Store in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
              </strategy>

              <strategy name="INDEX_GUIDED">
                <desc>Load index.md, analyze structure and description of each doc in the index, then intelligently load relevant docs</desc>
                <mandate>DO NOT BE LAZY - use best judgment to load documents that might have relevant information, even if only a 5% chance</mandate>
                <action>Load index.md from sharded directory</action>
                <action>Parse table of contents, links, section headers</action>
                <action>Analyze workflow's purpose and objective</action>
                <action>Identify which linked/referenced documents are likely relevant</action>
                <example>If workflow is about authentication and index shows "Auth Overview", "Payment Setup", "Deployment" → Load auth
                  docs, consider deployment docs, skip payment</example>
                <action>Load all identified relevant documents</action>
                <action>Store combined content in variable: {pattern_name_content}</action>
                <note>When in doubt, LOAD IT - context is valuable, being thorough is better than missing critical info</note>
              </strategy>
              <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2b" title="Try Whole Document if No Sharded Found">
            <check if="no sharded matches found OR no sharded pattern exists">
              <action>Attempt glob match on 'whole' pattern (e.g., "{output_folder}/*prd*.md")</action>
              <check if="matches found">
                <action>Load ALL matching files completely (no offset/limit)</action>
                <action>Store content in variable: {pattern_name_content} (e.g., {prd_content})</action>
                <action>Mark pattern as RESOLVED, skip to next pattern</action>
              </check>
            </check>
          </substep>

          <substep n="2c" title="Handle Not Found">
            <check if="no matches for sharded OR whole">
              <action>Set {pattern_name_content} to empty string</action>
              <action>Note in session: "No {pattern_name} files found" (not an error, just unavailable, offer use change to provide)</action>
            </check>
          </substep>
        </step>

        <step n="3" title="Report Discovery Results">
          <action>List all loaded content variables with file counts</action>
          <example>
            ✓ Loaded {prd_content} from 5 sharded files: prd/index.md, prd/requirements.md, ...
            ✓ Loaded {architecture_content} from 1 file: Architecture.md
            ✓ Loaded {epics_content} from selective load: epics/epic-3.md
            ○ No ux_design files found
          </example>
          <note>This gives workflow transparency into what context is available</note>
        </step>
      </flow>

    </protocol>
  </protocols>

  <llm final="true">
    <critical-rules>
      • This is the complete workflow execution engine

# Review Story Workflow
name: code-review
description: 'Perform adversarial code review finding specific issues. Use when the user says "run code review" or "review this code"'

# Critical variables from config
config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
user_skill_level: "{config_source}:user_skill_level"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
date: system-generated
planning_artifacts: "{config_source}:planning_artifacts"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"
sprint_status: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"

# Workflow components
installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/code-review"
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.xml"
validation: "{installed_path}/checklist.md"
template: false

project_context: "**/project-context.md"

# Smart input file references - handles both whole docs and sharded docs
# Priority: Whole document first, then sharded version
# Strategy: SELECTIVE LOAD - only load the specific epic needed for this story review
input_file_patterns:
  architecture:
    description: "System architecture for review context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  ux_design:
    description: "UX design specification (if UI review)"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  epics:
    description: "Epic containing story being reviewed"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md"
    sharded_index: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/index.md"
    sharded_single: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/epic-{{epic_num}}.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"

1→# Story 3.3: Parallel Subscription Processing
     2→
     3→Status: review
     4→
     5→<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
     6→
     7→## Story
     8→
     9→As a DevOps engineer managing many subscriptions,
    10→I want subscriptions to be processed in parallel using background jobs,
    11→so that the total execution time is closer to one subscription's time rather than the sum of all.
    12→
    13→## Acceptance Criteria
    14→
    15→1. **Given** a config file with 3 subscriptions
    16→   **When** the script processes them in parallel mode
    17→   **Then** all 3 subscriptions are processed concurrently using background jobs
    18→   **And** the script waits for all jobs to complete before printing the summary
    19→
    20→2. **Given** parallel processing is active and subscription "finops-infra" fails
    21→   **When** all jobs complete
    22→   **Then** the summary correctly reports the failure
    23→   **And** the other subscriptions' success/failure counts are accurate
    24→   **And** the exit code is 1 (any failure = non-zero)
    25→
    26→3. **Given** parallel execution with 3 subscriptions
    27→   **When** all 3 succeed
    28→   **Then** total wall-clock time is approximately 1 subscription's processing time (not 3x)
    29→   **And** log entries from different subscriptions may interleave but each line identifies the subscription
    30→
    31→4. **Given** the `--dry-run` flag is combined with parallel processing
    32→   **When** the script executes
    33→   **Then** dry-run validation runs in parallel as well (consistent behavior)
    34→
    35→## Tasks / Subtasks
    36→
    37→- [x] Task 1: Create per-subscription worker function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
    38→  - [x] 1.1: Create `process_subscription()` function that encapsulates the per-subscription work currently in the `while read -r sub` loop body (lines 681-726)
    39→  - [x] 1.2: Function signature: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file"` where `$result_file` is a temp file path for result communication
    40→  - [x] 1.3: Inside the function, iterate over models with the existing inner `while read -r model_entry` loop (process substitution preserved)
    41→  - [x] 1.4: Track `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total` counters inside the function
    42→  - [x] 1.5: On completion, write results to `$result_file` as a simple format: `success_count failed_count total_attempts`
    43→  - [x] 1.6: Function returns 0 if all models succeeded, 1 if any failed (for informational purposes — actual counting uses result files)
    44→
    45→- [x] Task 2: Refactor `main()` to launch background jobs (AC: #1, #3)
    46→  - [x] 2.1: Before the subscription loop, create a temp directory: `result_dir=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/quota-results-XXXXXX")`
    47→  - [x] 2.2: Add cleanup trap: `trap "rm -rf $result_dir" EXIT` (merge with any existing EXIT trap)
    48→  - [x] 2.3: Replace sequential loop body with: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &`
    49→  - [x] 2.4: Collect PIDs in an array: `pids+=($!)`
    50→  - [x] 2.5: After loop, `wait` for all PIDs: iterate `for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do wait "$pid" || true; done`
    51→  - [x] 2.6: After all jobs complete, aggregate results from all files in `$result_dir`
    52→
    53→- [x] Task 3: Result aggregation from temp files (AC: #2)
    54→  - [x] 3.1: After `wait`, iterate over result files in `$result_dir`
    55→  - [x] 3.2: For each file, read `success_count failed_count total_attempts` and sum into global counters
    56→  - [x] 3.3: Preserve existing counter integrity check: `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
    57→  - [x] 3.4: Handle edge case: if a result file is missing (job crashed), count all that subscription's models as failed
    58→
    59→- [x] Task 4: Handle `set -euo pipefail` compatibility with background jobs (AC: #1, #2)
    60→  - [x] 4.1: Background jobs inherit `set -e` but their failure does NOT kill the parent — verify this behavior
    61→  - [x] 4.2: In `process_subscription()`, ensure the function does NOT `exit` on failure (use `return` only) — `exit` in a background job kills only that subshell, but the result file must be written first
    62→  - [x] 4.3: Wrap the model retry loop in the function so that permanent failures are counted, NOT propagated as exits
    63→  - [x] 4.4: The `wait "$pid"` captures the exit code but we must `|| true` to prevent `set -e` from killing the parent
    64→
    65→- [x] Task 5: Ensure log readability with parallel output (AC: #3)
    66→  - [x] 5.1: The existing `log()` function already includes timestamps — no change needed for ordering
    67→  - [x] 5.2: All log messages inside `submit_quota_request()` already include `$sub_name` — verify this is sufficient for identification
    68→  - [x] 5.3: The `log()` function writes to stderr AND the log file via `tee -a` — concurrent writes to the same file may interleave mid-line
    69→  - [x] 5.4: Mitigate log interleaving: use `flock` on the log file for atomic writes: `(flock 200; echo -e "$formatted" >> "$LOG_FILE") 200>>"$LOG_FILE.lock"` — OR accept minor interleaving as documented behavior (simpler)
    70→  - [x] 5.5: Decision: Accept interleaving for MVP — each line is atomic in practice for short log messages via `echo`. Document this in the log file header comment.
    71→
    72→- [x] Task 6: Dry-run compatibility (AC: #4)
    73→  - [x] 6.1: No special handling needed — `DRY_RUN` is a global variable inherited by background jobs (Bash exports to subshells automatically for functions)
    74→  - [x] 6.2: Verify `submit_quota_request()` dry-run branch (lines 291-294) works identically when called from a background job
    75→  - [x] 6.3: Dry-run summary at lines 735-746 reads the same aggregated counters — no changes needed
    76→
    77→- [x] Task 7: Cleanup and edge cases (AC: #1, #2)
    78→  - [x] 7.1: Temp directory cleanup: `rm -rf "$result_dir"` in EXIT trap
    79→  - [x] 7.2: Handle 0 subscriptions selected: existing check at line 653-656 exits before parallel section — no change needed
    80→  - [x] 7.3: Handle 1 subscription: still launches as background job — overhead is negligible
    81→  - [x] 7.4: Malformed subscription handling: the existing check at lines 685-690 moves into `process_subscription()`, writes failed counts to result file
    82→
    83→- [x] Task 8: Regression testing (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
    84→  - [x] 8.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
    85→  - [x] 8.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings (SC2155 discipline: `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines)
    86→  - [x] 8.3: Verify `--dry-run` still works with multi-model config
    87→  - [x] 8.4: Verify `--all --dry-run` processes all subscriptions in parallel
    88→  - [x] 8.5: Verify `--help` output is still accurate
    89→  - [x] 8.6: Verify single-subscription config still works (trivial parallelism)
    90→  - [x] 8.7: Verify legacy single-model config still works with parallel processing
    91→
    92→## Dev Notes
    93→
    94→### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes
    95→
    96→The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is **765 lines**. Here are the exact locations for each change:
    97→
    98→**New `process_subscription()` function — add before `main()` (~line 598):**
    99→
   100→This function extracts the per-subscription processing logic currently inside the `while read -r sub` loop in `main()` (lines 680-727). It encapsulates:
   101→- Subscription field validation (lines 685-690)
   102→- Inner model loop with retry logic (lines 693-726)
   103→- Counter tracking per-subscription
   104→
   105→```bash
   106→# Process a single subscription's quota requests (all models)
   107→# Designed to run as a background job for parallel execution
   108→# Args: $1=subscription_json, $2=models_json, $3=result_file_path
   109→process_subscription() {
   110→    local sub_json=$1
   111→    local models=$2
   112→    local result_file=$3
   113→
   114→    local sub_id sub_name
   115→    sub_id=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.id')
   116→    sub_name=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.name')
   117→
   118→    local local_success=0
   119→    local local_failed=0
   120→    local local_total=0
   121→
   122→    # Validate subscription fields
   123→    if [[ -z "$sub_id" || "$sub_id" == "null" || -z "$sub_name" || "$sub_name" == "null" ]]; then
   124→        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Skipping malformed subscription entry (missing id or name)${NC}"
   125→        local_failed=$(echo "$models" | jq 'length')
   126→        local_total=$local_failed
   127→        echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
   128→        return 1
   129→    fi
   130→
   131→    # Inner loop: iterate over each model
   132→    while read -r model_entry; do
   133→        local_total=$((local_total + 1))
   134→        local retry_count=0
   135→        local rc=0
   136→
   137→        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
   138→            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" && rc=0 || rc=$?
   139→
   140→            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
   141→                local_success=$((local_success + 1))
   142→                break
   143→            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
   144→                local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
   145→                break
   146→            else
   147→                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
   148→                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   149→                    local model_label
   150→                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
   151→                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name ($model_label) in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
   152→                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   153→                else
   154→                    local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
   155→                    local model_label
   156→                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
   157→                    log "ERROR" "${RED}All $MAX_RETRIES retries exhausted for $sub_name ($model_label) — marking as failed${NC}"
   158→                fi
   159→            fi
   160→        done
   161→    done < <(echo "$models" | jq -c '.[]')
   162→
   163→    # Write results to temp file for aggregation
   164→    echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
   165→
   166→    if [[ $local_failed -gt 0 ]]; then
   167→        return 1
   168→    fi
   169→    return 0
   170→}
   171→```
   172→
   173→**`main()` — processing loop refactor (lines 671-727):**
   174→
   175→Replace the sequential loop with parallel background job dispatch:
   176→
   177→```bash
   178→# Current sequential code (lines 671-727):
   179→local success_count=0
   180→local failed_count=0
   181→local total_attempts=0
   182→# ... while read -r sub; do ... done
   183→
   184→# New parallel code:
   185→local result_dir
   186→result_dir=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/quota-results-XXXXXX")
   187→# Ensure cleanup on exit
   188→trap "rm -rf '$result_dir'" EXIT
   189→
   190→local pids=()
   191→local sub_index=0
   192→
   193→while read -r sub; do
   194→    local result_file="$result_dir/result-$sub_index"
   195→    process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &
   196→    pids+=($!)
   197→    sub_index=$((sub_index + 1))
   198→done < <(echo "$subs_json" | jq -c "$jq_filter")
   199→
   200→# Wait for all background jobs
   201→for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
   202→    wait "$pid" || true
   203→done
   204→
   205→# Aggregate results from temp files
   206→local success_count=0
   207→local failed_count=0
   208→local total_attempts=0
   209→
   210→for result_file in "$result_dir"/result-*; do
   211→    if [[ -f "$result_file" ]]; then
   212→        local s f t
   213→        read -r s f t < "$result_file"
   214→        success_count=$((success_count + s))
   215→        failed_count=$((failed_count + f))
   216→        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + t))
   217→    else
   218→        # Job crashed before writing results — count all models as failed
   219→        failed_count=$((failed_count + model_count))
   220→        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + model_count))
   221→    fi
   222→done
   223→```
   224→
   225→**Summary block (lines 729-760):** No changes needed — it reads the same `success_count`, `failed_count`, `total_attempts` variables.
   226→
   227→### Architecture Compliance
   228→
   229→| Rule | How This Story Complies |
   230→|------|------------------------|
   231→| `snake_case` for variables | `result_dir`, `result_file`, `sub_index`, `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total`, `process_subscription` |
   232→| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
   233→| `jq` for JSON construction | All JSON parsing via `jq -r`, `jq -c`, `jq 'length'` — no string concat |
   234→| No PII in logs | Log only subscription name/ID, model params — unchanged from current behavior |
   235→| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added. Background jobs inherit `set -e` but parent uses `wait "$pid" \|\| true` to prevent cascade |
   236→| Process substitution | Outer subscription loop uses `< <(...)`. Inner model loop inside `process_subscription()` also uses `< <(...)` |
   237→| Exit codes | 0=all success, 1=any failure — unchanged. Background job exit codes captured via result files, not `wait` return codes |
   238→
   239→### Bash Background Jobs — Key Technical Details
   240→
   241→**How background jobs work in Bash:**
   242→- `cmd &` forks a subshell that runs `cmd` — the subshell gets a COPY of all variables and functions
   243→- `$!` captures the PID of the last backgrounded job
   244→- `wait $pid` blocks until that job finishes and returns its exit code
   245→- With `set -e`, if `wait` returns non-zero, the parent would exit — hence `wait "$pid" || true`
   246→- Background subshells inherit functions (`submit_quota_request`, `log`, `classify_error`, etc.)
   247→- Background subshells inherit exported variables AND function-scope variables (since they're forked, not exec'd)
   248→- `LOG_FILE`, `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY`, `DRY_RUN`, `QUOTA_API_VERSION` — all accessible in background jobs
   249→
   250→**Result communication:**
   251→- Background jobs cannot modify parent shell variables (they're separate processes)
   252→- Solution: each job writes results to a unique temp file
   253→- Parent aggregates after all `wait` calls complete
   254→- Temp files are cleaned up via EXIT trap
   255→
   256→**Log file concurrency:**
   257→- Multiple background jobs write to the same `$LOG_FILE` via `echo ... >> "$LOG_FILE"`
   258→- Short `echo` writes (< 4096 bytes) are atomic on Linux/macOS per POSIX pipe_buf guarantee
   259→- Each log line is well under 4096 bytes — interleaving won't corrupt individual lines
   260→- Lines from different subscriptions may appear in any order — this is expected and documented
   261→- Console output (stderr) may also interleave — acceptable for parallel execution
   262→
   263→**`set -euo pipefail` behavior in background jobs:**
   264→- `-e` (errexit): If `submit_quota_request` returns non-zero, the retry loop handles it. The function must NOT let unhandled failures propagate to `set -e` outside the retry loop.
   265→- `-u` (nounset): All variables are properly initialized before use
   266→- `-o pipefail`: Affects pipes within the background job — the `jq -c '.[]'` pipe is fine since process substitution (`< <(...)`) is used instead of pipes
   267→
   268→### Parallel Processing Architecture
   269→
   270→```
   271→main()
   272→  ├── process_subscription(sub_1, models, /tmp/result-0) &  → PID 12345
   273→  ├── process_subscription(sub_2, models, /tmp/result-1) &  → PID 12346
   274→  └── process_subscription(sub_3, models, /tmp/result-2) &  → PID 12347
   275→      │
   276→      │   Each background job independently:
   277→      │   ├── validate subscription fields
   278→      │   ├── for each model:
   279→      │   │   ├── submit_quota_request() with retry
   280→      │   │   ├── classify_error() on failure
   281→      │   │   └── increment local counters
   282→      │   └── write "success failed total" to result file
   283→      │
   284→      wait PID 12345 || true
   285→      wait PID 12346 || true
   286→      wait PID 12347 || true
   287→      │
   288→      Aggregate: read all result-* files → sum counters
   289→      │
   290→      Print summary (same as before)
   291→```
   292→
   293→### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.2)
   294→
   295→1. **Normalize-once pattern works well.** Story 3.2 established `normalize_models_config()` — the parallel story does NOT change this. Models are normalized once in `main()`, then passed to each `process_subscription()` call.
   296→
   297→2. **Inner model loop stays sequential within each subscription.** Parallelism is at the SUBSCRIPTION level, not the model level. Each subscription processes its models sequentially. This avoids Azure subscription context conflicts (can't `az account set` to the same subscription concurrently from multiple processes).
   298→
   299→3. **Counter math: `total_attempts = subscriptions × models`.** This doesn't change — just the aggregation mechanism moves from direct variable increments to temp file summation.
   300→
   301→4. **SC2155 discipline: `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)`.** Continue this pattern in `process_subscription()`.
   302→
   303→5. **Documentation must be updated.** Story 3.2 review caught missing README updates. Update README.md to document parallel behavior.
   304→
   305→6. **`model_label` declared with `local` twice** was a LOW finding in 3.2 review — avoid this in the new function.
   306→
   307→7. **ShellCheck compliance.** Zero new warnings. Run `shellcheck -S info submit-quota-requests.sh`.
   308→
   309→8. **Malformed subscription handling** — the existing check at lines 685-690 already counts `model_count` as failed for a malformed sub. This moves into `process_subscription()` unchanged.
   310→
   311→### Git Intelligence (Recent Commits)
   312→
   313→- `2d90942 docs: deep rescan of project documentation (161→765 line script)` — Documentation was regenerated after 3.2. Script is 765 lines.
   314→- `bad6104 feat: update BMAD framework and enhance quota automation script` — Story 3.2 multi-model implementation.
   315→- `fd2e1e5 feat: add --dry-run flag` — Story 3.1. The `DRY_RUN` check is inside `submit_quota_request()` and will work in background jobs since the variable is inherited.
   316→- Work has been done in worktree branches (`worktree-bold-ray-60wn`). Same approach may be used.
   317→
   318→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   319→
   320→- **DO NOT** parallelize at the MODEL level within a subscription — `az account set` establishes a process-wide subscription context. Concurrent `az rest` calls for the same subscription are fine, but calling `az account set` from multiple background jobs creates a race condition. Parallel at SUBSCRIPTION level only.
   321→- **DO NOT** use shared variables for cross-job communication — background jobs are separate processes. Use temp files.
   322→- **DO NOT** use `&` on `submit_quota_request` directly — the retry logic must wrap it. Parallelize the entire `process_subscription` function, not individual API calls.
   323→- **DO NOT** add `set +e` anywhere — use `|| true` on `wait` and proper return codes in `process_subscription`.
   324→- **DO NOT** use `gnu parallel` or `xargs -P` — these add external dependencies. Bash `&` + `wait` is sufficient.
   325→- **DO NOT** pipe results through stdout — background jobs share stdout/stderr with the parent. Use temp files for structured data.
   326→- **DO NOT** modify `discover_subscriptions()`, `show_subscription_selector()`, TUI code, `classify_error()`, `validate_config()`, or `normalize_models_config()` — they are not affected.
   327→- **DO NOT** change the `--dry-run` or `--all` flag parsing — they work independently of parallelism.
   328→- **DO NOT** add a `--parallel` flag — parallel processing should be the default behavior. Sequential was never a feature, just an implementation limitation.
   329→- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function.
   330→
   331→### Edge Cases to Handle
   332→
   333→1. **Single subscription:** Still launches as background job. Overhead is negligible (~1ms fork). Result aggregation reads 1 file.
   334→2. **0 subscriptions selected:** Existing check at line 653 exits before the parallel section — no change needed.
   335→3. **Background job crashes (SIGKILL, OOM):** Result file won't be written. The aggregation loop detects missing files and counts `model_count` as failed for that subscription.

# Story 3.3: Parallel Subscription Processing

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a DevOps engineer managing many subscriptions,
I want subscriptions to be processed in parallel using background jobs,
so that the total execution time is closer to one subscription's time rather than the sum of all.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a config file with 3 subscriptions
   **When** the script processes them in parallel mode
   **Then** all 3 subscriptions are processed concurrently using background jobs
   **And** the script waits for all jobs to complete before printing the summary

2. **Given** parallel processing is active and subscription "finops-infra" fails
   **When** all jobs complete
   **Then** the summary correctly reports the failure
   **And** the other subscriptions' success/failure counts are accurate
   **And** the exit code is 1 (any failure = non-zero)

3. **Given** parallel execution with 3 subscriptions
   **When** all 3 succeed
   **Then** total wall-clock time is approximately 1 subscription's processing time (not 3x)
   **And** log entries from different subscriptions may interleave but each line identifies the subscription

4. **Given** the `--dry-run` flag is combined with parallel processing
   **When** the script executes
   **Then** dry-run validation runs in parallel as well (consistent behavior)

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create per-subscription worker function (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `process_subscription()` function that encapsulates the per-subscription work currently in the `while read -r sub` loop body (lines 681-726)
  - [x] 1.2: Function signature: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file"` where `$result_file` is a temp file path for result communication
  - [x] 1.3: Inside the function, iterate over models with the existing inner `while read -r model_entry` loop (process substitution preserved)
  - [x] 1.4: Track `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total` counters inside the function
  - [x] 1.5: On completion, write results to `$result_file` as a simple format: `success_count failed_count total_attempts`
  - [x] 1.6: Function returns 0 if all models succeeded, 1 if any failed (for informational purposes — actual counting uses result files)

- [x] Task 2: Refactor `main()` to launch background jobs (AC: #1, #3)
  - [x] 2.1: Before the subscription loop, create a temp directory: `result_dir=$(mktemp -d "/tmp/quota-results-XXXXXX")`
  - [x] 2.2: Add cleanup trap: `trap "rm -rf $result_dir" EXIT` (merge with any existing EXIT trap)
  - [x] 2.3: Replace sequential loop body with: `process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &`
  - [x] 2.4: Collect PIDs in an array: `pids+=($!)`
  - [x] 2.5: After loop, `wait` for all PIDs: iterate `for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do wait "$pid" || true; done`
  - [x] 2.6: After all jobs complete, aggregate results from all files in `$result_dir`

- [x] Task 3: Result aggregation from temp files (AC: #2)
  - [x] 3.1: After `wait`, iterate over result files in `$result_dir`
  - [x] 3.2: For each file, read `success_count failed_count total_attempts` and sum into global counters
  - [x] 3.3: Preserve existing counter integrity check: `success_count + failed_count == total_attempts`
  - [x] 3.4: Handle edge case: if a result file is missing (job crashed), count all that subscription's models as failed

- [x] Task 4: Handle `set -euo pipefail` compatibility with background jobs (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 4.1: Background jobs inherit `set -e` but their failure does NOT kill the parent — verify this behavior
  - [x] 4.2: In `process_subscription()`, ensure the function does NOT `exit` on failure (use `return` only) — `exit` in a background job kills only that subshell, but the result file must be written first
  - [x] 4.3: Wrap the model retry loop in the function so that permanent failures are counted, NOT propagated as exits
  - [x] 4.4: The `wait "$pid"` captures the exit code but we must `|| true` to prevent `set -e` from killing the parent

- [x] Task 5: Ensure log readability with parallel output (AC: #3)
  - [x] 5.1: The existing `log()` function already includes timestamps — no change needed for ordering
  - [x] 5.2: All log messages inside `submit_quota_request()` already include `$sub_name` — verify this is sufficient for identification
  - [x] 5.3: The `log()` function writes to stderr AND the log file via `tee -a` — concurrent writes to the same file may interleave mid-line
  - [x] 5.4: Mitigate log interleaving: use `flock` on the log file for atomic writes: `(flock 200; echo -e "$formatted" >> "$LOG_FILE") 200>>"$LOG_FILE.lock"` — OR accept minor interleaving as documented behavior (simpler)
  - [x] 5.5: Decision: Accept interleaving for MVP — each line is atomic in practice for short log messages via `echo`. Document this in the log file header comment.

- [x] Task 6: Dry-run compatibility (AC: #4)
  - [x] 6.1: No special handling needed — `DRY_RUN` is a global variable inherited by background jobs (Bash exports to subshells automatically for functions)
  - [x] 6.2: Verify `submit_quota_request()` dry-run branch (lines 291-294) works identically when called from a background job
  - [x] 6.3: Dry-run summary at lines 735-746 reads the same aggregated counters — no changes needed

- [x] Task 7: Cleanup and edge cases (AC: #1, #2)
  - [x] 7.1: Temp directory cleanup: `rm -rf "$result_dir"` in EXIT trap
  - [x] 7.2: Handle 0 subscriptions selected: existing check at line 653-656 exits before parallel section — no change needed
  - [x] 7.3: Handle 1 subscription: still launches as background job — overhead is negligible
  - [x] 7.4: Malformed subscription handling: the existing check at lines 685-690 moves into `process_subscription()`, writes failed counts to result file

- [x] Task 8: Regression testing (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 8.1: `bash -n submit-quota-requests.sh` — exit code 0
  - [x] 8.2: ShellCheck — no new warnings (SC2155 discipline: `local var` then `var=$(...)` on separate lines)
  - [x] 8.3: Verify `--dry-run` still works with multi-model config
  - [x] 8.4: Verify `--all --dry-run` processes all subscriptions in parallel
  - [x] 8.5: Verify `--help` output is still accurate
  - [x] 8.6: Verify single-subscription config still works (trivial parallelism)
  - [x] 8.7: Verify legacy single-model config still works with parallel processing

## Dev Notes

### Current Script State — Where to Make Changes

The script `submit-quota-requests.sh` is **765 lines**. Here are the exact locations for each change:

**New `process_subscription()` function — add before `main()` (~line 598):**

This function extracts the per-subscription processing logic currently inside the `while read -r sub` loop in `main()` (lines 680-727). It encapsulates:
- Subscription field validation (lines 685-690)
- Inner model loop with retry logic (lines 693-726)
- Counter tracking per-subscription

```bash
# Process a single subscription's quota requests (all models)
# Designed to run as a background job for parallel execution
# Args: $1=subscription_json, $2=models_json, $3=result_file_path
process_subscription() {
    local sub_json=$1
    local models=$2
    local result_file=$3

    local sub_id sub_name
    sub_id=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.id')
    sub_name=$(echo "$sub_json" | jq -r '.name')

    local local_success=0
    local local_failed=0
    local local_total=0

    # Validate subscription fields
    if [[ -z "$sub_id" || "$sub_id" == "null" || -z "$sub_name" || "$sub_name" == "null" ]]; then
        log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Skipping malformed subscription entry (missing id or name)${NC}"
        local_failed=$(echo "$models" | jq 'length')
        local_total=$local_failed
        echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Inner loop: iterate over each model
    while read -r model_entry; do
        local_total=$((local_total + 1))
        local retry_count=0
        local rc=0

        while [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
            submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$model_entry" && rc=0 || rc=$?

            if [[ $rc -eq 0 ]]; then
                local_success=$((local_success + 1))
                break
            elif [[ $rc -eq 1 ]]; then
                local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
                break
            else
                retry_count=$((retry_count + 1))
                if [[ $retry_count -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
                    local model_label
                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
                    log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $retry_count/$MAX_RETRIES for $sub_name ($model_label) in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
                    sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
                else
                    local_failed=$((local_failed + 1))
                    local model_label
                    model_label=$(echo "$model_entry" | jq -r '.model_name // .model')
                    log "ERROR" "${RED}All $MAX_RETRIES retries exhausted for $sub_name ($model_label) — marking as failed${NC}"
                fi
            fi
        done
    done < <(echo "$models" | jq -c '.[]')

    # Write results to temp file for aggregation
    echo "$local_success $local_failed $local_total" > "$result_file"

    if [[ $local_failed -gt 0 ]]; then
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}
```

**`main()` — processing loop refactor (lines 671-727):**

Replace the sequential loop with parallel background job dispatch:

```bash
# Current sequential code (lines 671-727):
local success_count=0
local failed_count=0
local total_attempts=0
# ... while read -r sub; do ... done

# New parallel code:
local result_dir
result_dir=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/quota-results-XXXXXX")
# Ensure cleanup on exit
trap "rm -rf '$result_dir'" EXIT

local pids=()
local sub_index=0

while read -r sub; do
    local result_file="$result_dir/result-$sub_index"
    process_subscription "$sub" "$models_json" "$result_file" &
    pids+=($!)
    sub_index=$((sub_index + 1))
done < <(echo "$subs_json" | jq -c "$jq_filter")

# Wait for all background jobs
for pid in "${pids[@]}"; do
    wait "$pid" || true
done

# Aggregate results from temp files
local success_count=0
local failed_count=0
local total_attempts=0

for result_file in "$result_dir"/result-*; do
    if [[ -f "$result_file" ]]; then
        local s f t
        read -r s f t < "$result_file"
        success_count=$((success_count + s))
        failed_count=$((failed_count + f))
        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + t))
    else
        # Job crashed before writing results — count all models as failed
        failed_count=$((failed_count + model_count))
        total_attempts=$((total_attempts + model_count))
    fi
done
```

**Summary block (lines 729-760):** No changes needed — it reads the same `success_count`, `failed_count`, `total_attempts` variables.

### Architecture Compliance

| Rule | How This Story Complies |
|------|------------------------|
| `snake_case` for variables | `result_dir`, `result_file`, `sub_index`, `local_success`, `local_failed`, `local_total`, `process_subscription` |
| `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants | No new constants needed |
| `jq` for JSON construction | All JSON parsing via `jq -r`, `jq -c`, `jq 'length'` — no string concat |
| No PII in logs | Log only subscription name/ID, model params — unchanged from current behavior |
| `set -euo pipefail` preserved | No `set +e` escapes added. Background jobs inherit `set -e` but parent uses `wait "$pid" \|\| true` to prevent cascade |
| Process substitution | Outer subscription loop uses `< <(...)`. Inner model loop inside `process_subscription()` also uses `< <(...)` |
| Exit codes | 0=all success, 1=any failure — unchanged. Background job exit codes captured via result files, not `wait` return codes |

### Bash Background Jobs — Key Technical Details

**How background jobs work in Bash:**
- `cmd &` forks a subshell that runs `cmd` — the subshell gets a COPY of all variables and functions
- `$!` captures the PID of the last backgrounded job
- `wait $pid` blocks until that job finishes and returns its exit code
- With `set -e`, if `wait` returns non-zero, the parent would exit — hence `wait "$pid" || true`
- Background subshells inherit functions (`submit_quota_request`, `log`, `classify_error`, etc.)
- Background subshells inherit exported variables AND function-scope variables (since they're forked, not exec'd)
- `LOG_FILE`, `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY`, `DRY_RUN`, `QUOTA_API_VERSION` — all accessible in background jobs

**Result communication:**
- Background jobs cannot modify parent shell variables (they're separate processes)
- Solution: each job writes results to a unique temp file
- Parent aggregates after all `wait` calls complete
- Temp files are cleaned up via EXIT trap

**Log file concurrency:**
- Multiple background jobs write to the same `$LOG_FILE` via `echo ... >> "$LOG_FILE"`
- Short `echo` writes (< 4096 bytes) are atomic on Linux/macOS per POSIX pipe_buf guarantee
- Each log line is well under 4096 bytes — interleaving won't corrupt individual lines
- Lines from different subscriptions may appear in any order — this is expected and documented
- Console output (stderr) may also interleave — acceptable for parallel execution

**`set -euo pipefail` behavior in background jobs:**
- `-e` (errexit): If `submit_quota_request` returns non-zero, the retry loop handles it. The function must NOT let unhandled failures propagate to `set -e` outside the retry loop.
- `-u` (nounset): All variables are properly initialized before use
- `-o pipefail`: Affects pipes within the background job — the `jq -c '.[]'` pipe is fine since process substitution (`< <(...)`) is used instead of pipes

### Parallel Processing Architecture

```
main()
  ├── process_subscription(sub_1, models, /tmp/result-0) &  → PID 12345
  ├── process_subscription(sub_2, models, /tmp/result-1) &  → PID 12346
  └── process_subscription(sub_3, models, /tmp/result-2) &  → PID 12347
      │
      │   Each background job independently:
      │   ├── validate subscription fields
      │   ├── for each model:
      │   │   ├── submit_quota_request() with retry
      │   │   ├── classify_error() on failure
      │   │   └── increment local counters
      │   └── write "success failed total" to result file
      │
      wait PID 12345 || true
      wait PID 12346 || true
      wait PID 12347 || true
      │
      Aggregate: read all result-* files → sum counters
      │
      Print summary (same as before)
```

### Previous Story Intelligence (Story 3.2)

1. **Normalize-once pattern works well.** Story 3.2 established `normalize_models_config()` — the parallel story does NOT change this. Models are normalized once in `main()`, then passed to each `process_subscription()` call.

2. **Inner model loop stays sequential within each subscription.** Parallelism is at the SUBSCRIPTION level, not the model level. Each subscription processes its models sequentially. This avoids Azure subscription context conflicts (can't `az account set` to the same subscription concurrently from multiple processes).

3. **Counter math: `total_attempts = subscriptions × models`.** This doesn't change — just the aggregation mechanism moves from direct variable increments to temp file summation.

4. **SC2155 discipline: `local var` separate from `var=$(cmd)`.** Continue this pattern in `process_subscription()`.

5. **Documentation must be updated.** Story 3.2 review caught missing README updates. Update README.md to document parallel behavior.

6. **`model_label` declared with `local` twice** was a LOW finding in 3.2 review — avoid this in the new function.

7. **ShellCheck compliance.** Zero new warnings. Run `shellcheck -S info submit-quota-requests.sh`.

8. **Malformed subscription handling** — the existing check at lines 685-690 already counts `model_count` as failed for a malformed sub. This moves into `process_subscription()` unchanged.

### Git Intelligence (Recent Commits)

- `2d90942 docs: deep rescan of project documentation (161→765 line script)` — Documentation was regenerated after 3.2. Script is 765 lines.
- `bad6104 feat: update BMAD framework and enhance quota automation script` — Story 3.2 multi-model implementation.
- `fd2e1e5 feat: add --dry-run flag` — Story 3.1. The `DRY_RUN` check is inside `submit_quota_request()` and will work in background jobs since the variable is inherited.
- Work has been done in worktree branches (`worktree-bold-ray-60wn`). Same approach may be used.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- **DO NOT** parallelize at the MODEL level within a subscription — `az account set` establishes a process-wide subscription context. Concurrent `az rest` calls for the same subscription are fine, but calling `az account set` from multiple background jobs creates a race condition. Parallel at SUBSCRIPTION level only.
- **DO NOT** use shared variables for cross-job communication — background jobs are separate processes. Use temp files.
- **DO NOT** use `&` on `submit_quota_request` directly — the retry logic must wrap it. Parallelize the entire `process_subscription` function, not individual API calls.
- **DO NOT** add `set +e` anywhere — use `|| true` on `wait` and proper return codes in `process_subscription`.
- **DO NOT** use `gnu parallel` or `xargs -P` — these add external dependencies. Bash `&` + `wait` is sufficient.
- **DO NOT** pipe results through stdout — background jobs share stdout/stderr with the parent. Use temp files for structured data.
- **DO NOT** modify `discover_subscriptions()`, `show_subscription_selector()`, TUI code, `classify_error()`, `validate_config()`, or `normalize_models_config()` — they are not affected.
- **DO NOT** change the `--dry-run` or `--all` flag parsing — they work independently of parallelism.
- **DO NOT** add a `--parallel` flag — parallel processing should be the default behavior. Sequential was never a feature, just an implementation limitation.
- **DO NOT** use `echo` for user-facing output — always use the `log()` function.

### Edge Cases to Handle

1. **Single subscription:** Still launches as background job. Overhead is negligible (~1ms fork). Result aggregation reads 1 file.
2. **0 subscriptions selected:** Existing check at line 653 exits before the parallel section — no change needed.
3. **Background job crashes (SIGKILL, OOM):** Result file won't be written. The aggregation loop detects missing files and counts `model_count` as failed for that subscription.
4. **Temp directory creation fails:** `mktemp -d` will fail with `set -e` active — script exits with error. This is correct behavior.
5. **Very many subscriptions (50+):** All launch simultaneously. Bash can handle hundreds of background jobs. Azure rate limiting (429) is handled by retry logic within each job.
6. **Log file contention:** Short `echo` writes are atomic. Worst case: two lines appear adjacent without newline separation. This is extremely rare for sub-4096-byte writes and acceptable.
7. **EXIT trap collision:** The existing TUI code sets an EXIT trap (line 565) but clears it at line 584. The parallel cleanup trap (`rm -rf "$result_dir"`) should be set AFTER TUI completion, which it is since TUI runs before the processing loop.
8. **`umask 077` (line 8):** Applies to temp files created by `mktemp` — result files are protected. Good.

- Only `submit-quota-requests.sh` gets functional code changes
- No new files created
- No new dependencies introduced
- Alignment with project structure: all changes in repo root (script only)

    29→# Architecture Decision Document — azure-quota-automation
    30→
    31→**Author:** Barbosa
    32→**Date:** 2026-02-13
    33→
    34→## Project Context Analysis
    35→
    36→### Requirements Overview
    37→
    38→**Functional Requirements:**
    39→
    40→The PRD defines 17 functional requirements across 4 capability areas:
    41→
    42→| Category | FRs | Architectural Impact |
    43→|---|---|---|
    44→| **Quota Request Submission** | FR1–FR5 | Core execution loop — Azure Quota API integration, subscription context switching, retry logic, sequential processing |
    45→| **Configuration Management** | FR6–FR9 | JSON config loading, path resolution, schema validation via `jq` |
    46→| **Reporting & Logging** | FR10–FR14 | Dual-output logging (console + file), color-coded console, accurate counters, exit code logic |
    47→| **Dependency & Environment Validation** | FR15–FR17 | Pre-flight checks for `az`, `jq`, `curl`; Azure CLI auth validation |
    48→
    49→The most architecturally significant requirements are:
    50→- **FR1 (Azure Quota API integration):** The primary gap — the current script simulates this call. This is the core MVP deliverable.
    51→- **FR3 (Retry logic):** Already partially implemented but must work correctly with the real API response codes.
    52→- **FR13 (Accurate counters):** Blocked by a subshell scoping bug that must be fixed at the architectural level (not a simple code fix).
    53→
    54→**Non-Functional Requirements:**
    55→
    56→12 NFRs across 4 categories drive architectural decisions:
    57→
    58→| Category | NFRs | Architectural Constraint |
    59→|---|---|---|
    60→| **Security** | NFR1–NFR4 | Config file permissions (`chmod 600`), no credential storage, `az login` session only, no PII in logs |
    61→| **Reliability** | NFR5–NFR7 | `set -euo pipefail`, 3-retry with 5s delay, fault isolation per subscription |
    62→| **Performance** | NFR8–NFR9 | 3 subscriptions in <60s (excluding retries), fixed 5s retry delay |
    63→| **Integration** | NFR10–NFR12 | macOS + Linux compatibility, Azure CLI latest, standard Unix utilities only |
    64→
    65→**Scale & Complexity:**
    66→
    67→- Primary domain: CLI automation / infrastructure tooling
    68→- Complexity level: Low
    69→- Estimated architectural components: 4 (script, config, API integration, logging)
    70→- Target scale: 3 subscriptions (Hypera Pharma), extensible to more
    71→
    72→### Technical Constraints & Dependencies
    73→
    74→| Constraint | Detail |
    75→|---|---|
    76→| **Language** | Bash 4.0+ (brownfield — existing 161-line script) |
    77→| **External deps** | Azure CLI (`az`), `jq`, `curl` — no additional deps allowed for MVP |
    78→| **Auth model** | User's `az login` session — no service principals or managed identities for MVP |
    79→| **API target** | Azure Quota API (REST) for AI model capacity requests |
    80→| **Platform** | macOS (primary dev), Linux (CI/CD target) |
    81→| **Config format** | JSON (`quota-config.json`) — flat structure, no nesting beyond subscription array |
    82→
    83→### Cross-Cutting Concerns Identified
    84→
    85→1. **Subshell variable scoping** — The `while read` pipeline creates a subshell, causing counter increments to be lost. Affects FR13 (accurate reporting) and FR14 (exit codes). Must be solved at the process level, not patched.
    86→2. **Error isolation** — NFR7 requires that one subscription failure does not halt others. The current `set -e` behavior must be carefully managed within the retry loop.
    87→3. **Logging consistency** — FR10–FR12 require every operation to be logged with timestamp, level, and message to both console and file simultaneously.
    88→4. **Security hygiene** — NFR1–NFR4 require strict separation between what gets logged (subscription IDs = OK) and what must not be logged (PII fields, tokens).
    89→
    90→## Starter Template Evaluation
    91→
    92→### Primary Technology Domain
    93→
    94→**CLI tool (Bash)** — This is a brownfield project with an existing 161-line Bash script. The project type is `cli_tool` with `general` domain and `low` complexity.
    95→
    96→### Starter Options Considered
    97→
    98→| Option | Assessment |
    99→|---|---|
   100→| **Keep existing Bash script** | Preferred. Script is functional, well-structured, and only needs 2 targeted fixes (API call + counter bug). Rewriting adds risk without value. |
   101→| **Rewrite in Python (Click/Typer)** | Rejected. Over-engineering for the current scope. Introduces a new runtime dependency. The team is Bash-native. |
   102→| **Rewrite in Go (Cobra)** | Rejected. Compiled binary would simplify distribution but is overkill for 3-subscription batch processing. |
   103→| **oclif (TypeScript CLI)** | Rejected. Heavyweight framework for what is a single-command script with no subcommands. |
   104→
   105→### Selected Starter: Existing Bash Script (Brownfield Enhancement)
   106→
   107→**Rationale for Selection:**
   108→- The existing `submit-quota-requests.sh` already implements 80% of the required functionality
   109→- Dependencies (`az`, `jq`, `curl`) are already validated and used correctly
   110→- Config validation, logging, dependency checking, and retry logic are all working
   111→- Only 2 critical changes are needed: (1) real API call, (2) counter bug fix
   112→- Rewriting introduces risk, delay, and new testing requirements with zero user benefit
   113→
   114→**Initialization Command:**
   115→
   116→```bash
   117→# No initialization needed — brownfield enhancement of existing script
   118→# The existing submit-quota-requests.sh is the starting point
   119→```
   120→
   121→**Architectural Decisions Provided by Existing Script:**
   122→
   123→- **Language & Runtime:** Bash 4.0+ with `set -euo pipefail`
   124→- **Config Format:** JSON parsed via `jq`
   125→- **Logging:** `tee -a` to both console and log file with timestamps
   126→- **Retry:** Fixed 3-attempt retry with 5-second delay
   127→- **Auth:** Azure CLI session via `az account set`
   128→- **Output:** Color-coded console (ANSI escape codes) + plain-text log file
   129→
   130→**Note:** No project initialization story is needed. The first implementation story is the API integration fix.
   131→
   132→## Core Architectural Decisions
   133→
   134→### Decision Priority Analysis
   135→
   136→**Critical Decisions (Block Implementation):**
   137→
   138→1. Azure Quota API call mechanism (`az rest` vs `curl`)
   139→2. Subshell counter bug fix strategy (process substitution vs temp file vs `for` loop)
   140→3. API response validation approach
   141→
   142→**Important Decisions (Shape Architecture):**
   143→
   144→4. Error classification (transient vs permanent failures)
   145→5. Config-driven vs `az account list`-driven subscription iteration
   146→6. Log file content filtering (PII exclusion)
   147→
   148→**Deferred Decisions (Post-MVP):**
   149→
   150→- Dry-run mode implementation
   151→- Parallel subscription processing
   152→- Multi-model config schema
   153→- Output format options (JSON/table)
   154→
   155→### API Integration Architecture
   156→
   157→**Decision:** Use `az rest` for Azure Quota API calls
   158→
   159→**Rationale:**
   160→- `az rest` automatically handles authentication using the active `az login` session (NFR2, NFR3)
   161→- No need to manually extract and manage Bearer tokens
   162→- Consistent with the existing `az account set` pattern in the script
   163→- Handles Azure resource manager endpoint routing automatically
   164→- Falls back to `curl` only if `az rest` proves insufficient for the specific quota endpoint
   165→
   166→**API Call Pattern:**
   167→
   168→```bash
   169→az rest \
   170→  --method PUT \
   171→  --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/${sub_id}/providers/Microsoft.Capacity/resourceProviders/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/locations/${region}/serviceLimits/${resource_name}?api-version=2023-02-01" \
   172→  --body "${request_body}"
   173→```
   174→
   175→**Alternative considered:** Direct `curl` with Bearer token extraction via `az account get-access-token`. Rejected because it adds token management complexity and expiration handling.
   176→
   177→### Subshell Counter Fix
   178→
   179→**Decision:** Replace `while read` pipeline with process substitution
   180→
   181→**Rationale:**
   182→- The current `echo "$subscriptions" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r sub` creates a subshell, so `success_count` and `failed_count` increments are lost when the pipeline exits
   183→- Process substitution (`while read -r sub; do ... done < <(...)`) keeps the loop body in the main shell
   184→- This is the idiomatic Bash solution with zero structural impact on the rest of the script
   185→- Alternative (temp file) adds cleanup complexity; alternative (`for` loop with `readarray`) changes the iteration pattern unnecessarily
   186→
   187→**Implementation:**
   188→
   189→```bash
   190→# BEFORE (broken — subshell loses counter values):
   191→echo "$subscriptions" | jq -c '.[]' | while read -r sub; do ...
   192→
   193→# AFTER (fixed — loop runs in main shell):
   194→while read -r sub; do
   195→    ...
   196→done < <(echo "$config" | jq -c '.subscriptions[]')
   197→```
   198→
   199→### Subscription Iteration Source
   200→
   201→**Decision:** Iterate from config file subscriptions, not `az account list`
   202→
   203→**Rationale:**
   204→- The PRD specifies 3 target subscriptions in the config (FR5, FR9)
   205→- `az account list` returns ALL accessible subscriptions, which may include unrelated ones
   206→- Config-driven iteration gives explicit control over which subscriptions receive quota requests
   207→- The current script uses `az account list` — this is a bug, not a feature
   208→
   209→**Change:** Replace `az account list` call with `jq '.subscriptions[]' "$CONFIG_FILE"` iteration.
   210→
   211→### Error Classification
   212→
   213→**Decision:** Classify API errors as transient (retry) or permanent (fail immediately)
   214→
   215→| Error Type | HTTP Codes | Action |
   216→|---|---|---|
   217→| **Transient** | 429 (rate limit), 503 (service unavailable), 504 (gateway timeout), network errors | Retry up to 3 times with 5s delay |
   218→| **Permanent** | 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthorized), 403 (forbidden), 404 (not found), 409 (conflict) | Log error, skip to next subscription |
   219→| **Success** | 200, 201, 202 | Log success, increment counter |
   220→
   221→**Rationale:** Retrying permanent errors wastes time and clutters logs. The existing script retries all failures indiscriminately.
   222→
   223→### Authentication & Security
   224→
   225→**Decision:** Rely exclusively on `az login` session with pre-flight validation
   226→
   227→- **Pre-flight check:** `az account show` to verify active session before processing
   228→- **No credentials in config:** NFR2 — config file contains only PII and Azure resource identifiers
   229→- **PII protection:** Config file must be `chmod 600` (NFR1); log files must NOT echo PII fields (name, email, address) — only subscription ID/name and model parameters
   230→- **Log file permissions:** Generated log files should be created with `umask 077` to restrict access
   231→
   232→### Infrastructure & Deployment
   233→
   234→**Decision:** No deployment infrastructure for MVP — local execution only
   235→
   236→- Script runs from a cloned repository on developer workstations
   237→- No CI/CD pipeline, Docker container, or package distribution for MVP
   238→- Future phase: Azure DevOps pipeline task wrapper (PRD Vision)
   239→- Future phase: `brew tap` or `curl | bash` installation (not in scope)
   240→
   241→## Implementation Patterns & Consistency Rules
   242→
   243→### Naming Patterns
   244→
   245→**Script Functions:**
   246→- `snake_case` for all function names: `submit_quota_request`, `check_dependencies`, `validate_config`
   247→- Prefix helper functions with their scope: `log`, `error_exit`
   248→
   249→**Variables:**
   250→- `snake_case` for all variables: `sub_id`, `sub_name`, `success_count`, `retry_count`
   251→- `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants and config: `CONFIG_FILE`, `LOG_FILE`, `MAX_RETRIES`, `RETRY_DELAY`
   252→- Color constants: `RED`, `GREEN`, `YELLOW`, `BLUE`, `NC`
   253→
   254→**Log Messages:**
   255→- Format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS [LEVEL] message`
   256→- Levels: `INFO`, `WARN`, `ERROR` (no DEBUG for MVP)
   257→- Include subscription name in all per-subscription messages
   258→- Never include PII (name, email, address) in log messages
   259→
   260→### Structure Patterns
   261→
   262→**Function Order in Script:**
   263→1. Shebang and header comments
   264→2. `set -euo pipefail`
   265→3. Configuration constants
   266→4. Color constants
   267→5. Utility functions (`log`, `error_exit`)
   268→6. Validation functions (`check_dependencies`, `validate_config`)
   269→7. Core business logic (`submit_quota_request`)
   270→8. Main orchestration (`main`)
   271→9. Script entry point (`main "$@"`)
   272→
   273→**Error Handling Pattern:**
   274→```bash
   275→# For API calls — capture response and HTTP code
   276→local response
   277→local http_code
   278→response=$(az rest ... 2>&1) && http_code=$? || http_code=$?
   279→
   280→if [[ $http_code -eq 0 ]]; then
   281→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}SUCCESS: Quota request submitted for $sub_name${NC}"
   282→    return 0
   283→else
   284→    log "ERROR" "${RED}FAILED: $response${NC}"
   285→    return 1
   286→fi
   287→```
   288→
   289→**Retry Pattern:**
   290→```bash
   291→local attempt=0
   292→while [[ $attempt -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; do
   293→    if submit_quota_request "$sub_id" "$sub_name" "$config"; then
   294→        ((success_count++))
   295→        break
   296→    else
   297→        ((attempt++))
   298→        if [[ $attempt -lt $MAX_RETRIES ]]; then
   299→            log "WARN" "${YELLOW}Retry $attempt/$MAX_RETRIES in ${RETRY_DELAY}s...${NC}"
   300→            sleep "$RETRY_DELAY"
   301→        else
   302→            ((failed_count++))
   303→            log "ERROR" "${RED}Failed after $MAX_RETRIES attempts: $sub_name${NC}"
   304→        fi
   305→    fi
   306→done
   307→```
   308→
   309→### Format Patterns
   310→
   311→**JSON Request Body Construction:**
   312→- Use `jq` to build JSON payloads — never string concatenation
   313→- Template: `jq -n --arg name "$name" --arg capacity "$capacity" '{...}'`
   314→
   315→**API Response Handling:**
   316→- Parse response JSON with `jq` to extract status and error messages
   317→- Log the full response body at INFO level (excluding any tokens)
   318→- Extract user-facing error message for WARN/ERROR log entries
   319→
   320→**Log File Format:**
   321→```
   322→2026-02-13 14:30:22 [INFO] === Azure AI Quota Request Automation ===
   323→2026-02-13 14:30:22 [INFO] Processing subscription: infrastructure-dev (e214e4b4-...)
   324→2026-02-13 14:30:23 [INFO] SUCCESS: Quota request submitted for infrastructure-dev
   325→2026-02-13 14:30:23 [INFO] === Submission Summary ===
   326→2026-02-13 14:30:23 [INFO] Successful: 3 | Failed: 0
   327→```
   328→
   329→### Process Patterns
   330→
   331→**Exit Code Logic:**
   332→```bash
   333→if [[ $failed_count -gt 0 ]]; then
   334→    log "ERROR" "${RED}$failed_count subscription(s) failed${NC}"
   335→    exit 1
   336→else
   337→    log "INFO" "${GREEN}All $success_count subscription(s) succeeded${NC}"
   338→    exit 0
   339→fi
   340→```
   341→
   342→**Config Validation Pattern:**
   343→- Check file exists → Check valid JSON (`jq empty`) → Check required fields present
   344→- Required fields: `model`, `region`, `capacity`, `subscriptions` (array with at least 1 element)
   345→- Each subscription must have `id` and `name`
   346→
   347→### Enforcement Guidelines
   348→
   349→**All AI Agents MUST:**
   350→
   351→1. Follow `snake_case` for functions and variables, `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants
   352→2. Use `jq` for all JSON construction and parsing — never use string interpolation for JSON
   353→3. Log every API call attempt and result with the subscription name
   354→4. Never log PII fields (firstName, lastName, email, address, company)
   355→5. Use process substitution (`< <(...)`) instead of pipe-based iteration
   356→6. Return proper exit codes from functions (0=success, 1=failure)
   357→7. Preserve `set -euo pipefail` behavior — never add `set +e` escapes
   358→
   359→**Anti-Patterns:**
   360→
   361→- `echo "$json" | curl -d @-` — Use `az rest --body` instead
   362→- `eval` or backtick command substitution — Use `$()` consistently
   363→- Hardcoded subscription IDs in the script — Always read from config
   364→- `grep` for JSON parsing — Always use `jq`
   365→- Color codes in log file output — Only in console output

   394→### Architectural Boundaries
   395→
   396→**Script Boundaries:**
   397→
   398→The script has 3 clear phases with strict boundaries:
   399→
   400→| Phase | Functions | Failure Mode |
   401→|---|---|---|
   402→| **Pre-flight** | `check_dependencies()`, `validate_config()`, auth check | Hard exit (`error_exit`) — no point continuing |
   403→| **Execution** | `submit_quota_request()` per subscription with retry | Isolated failure — log and continue to next subscription |
